data.title: Sibanye Stillwater to sell uranium-rich Beatrix shaft to Neo Energy in $28M deal
data.description:
Sibanye Stillwater (NYSE:SBSW) +9.4% in Monday's trading after saying it agreed to sell its Beatrix 4 shaft, which includes the Beisa uranium project, to Neo Energy Metals for 500M South African rand (~$28M) in cash and shares.

The move grants Sibanye (SBSW) a 40% equity stake in Neo Energy, positioning it as the company's largest shareholder and ensuring continued exposure to the uranium market.

Sibanye (SBSW) placed the Beatrix 4 shaft on care and maintenance in 2023 due to dwindling gold reserves and weak uranium prices, but uranium prices have since rebounded, reaching as high as $106/lb in January 2024.

Despite uranium's price recovery, Sibanye (SBSW) did not prioritize capital investment in the Beisa project, viewing the sale as a strategic opportunity to allow Neo Energy to advance the asset's development.

data.title: Consumers' inflation expectations tick up in NY Fed survey, but so does earnings outlook
data.description:
American consumers' inflation expectations ticked up against three time horizons, while their outlook for their own earnings growth over the next year increased slightly more, according to the Federal Reserve Bank of New York's November 2024 Survey of Consumer Expectations, released on Monday. In addition, for the most part, they expect to keep up with debt payments over the next three months.

Median inflation expectations rose 0.1 percentage point at all three time horizons — one year, three years, and five years. The median year-ahead inflation expectation edged up to 3.0%, three-year-ahead increased to 2.6%, and five-year ahead climbed to 2.9%. However, uncertainty over median inflation outcomes also increased across all three timeframes.

Median one-year-ahead earnings growth expectations rose 0.2 pp to 3.0% in November, still within a range of 2.7%-3.0% that the metric has kept at since January 2024.

Mean unemployment expectations — or the mean probability that the U.S. unemployment rate will be higher a year from now, rose 0.5 pp to 35.0% in November, still below its trailing 12-month average of 37.0%. Consumers were also slightly more worried about their own jobs. The mean perceived probability of losing one's job in the next 12 months increased by 0.5 pp to 13.5%.

Household income growth expectations, though, are slightly lower than the earnings growth outlook, rising by 0.1 pp to 3.1% in November. That series has been holding within a 2.9%-3.3% band since January 2023.

Median household spending growth expectations dipped 0.2 pp to 4.7%, the lowest reading since April 2021, but above prepandemic levels.

The average perceived probability of missing a minimum debt payment over the next three months dropped by 0.7 pp to 13.2%, the lowest reading since June 2024.

On Friday, the University of Michigan's Consumer Sentiment Index rose for the fifth straight month, as consumers see now as a good time to buy durables to avoid future price increases. That survey's year-ahead inflation expectation rose to 2.9% in December from 2.6% in November.

data.title: Volato Group withdraws S-1 registration statement, anticipates positive Q4 net income
data.description:
• Volato Group (NYSE:SOAR) announced the withdrawal of its S-1 registration statement. 
  • The company remains on track to deliver positive net income in Q4 2024.
  • SOAR shares rose over 8% on Monday. 
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Logility Supply Chain gains as a holder calls for review of strategic alternatives
data.description:
Logility Supply Chain Solutions (NASDAQ:LGTY) rose 1% after a shareholder called on the company to explore strategic alternatives, including a sale of the company.

2717 Partners LP is calling for the company to evaluate alternatives, including a sale, the holder wrote in a letter to Logility's (LGTY) board on Monday.

"We believe that if the status quo persists and Logility continues to operate in the public markets, the Company will be unable to achieve its long-term goals for all stakeholders," Edward Robson, 2717 Partners' Chief Investment Officer wrote in the letter. "Logility should find a new home in the private markets."

Shares of Logility (LGTY) fell 2.7% this year through Friday.

data.title: Orion Energy Systems partners with energy management service provider
data.description:
• Orion Energy Systems (NASDAQ:OESX) announced a partnership with an energy management service provider that serves over 6,500 customer locations across the county.
  • Orion expects the partnership to generate revenue of $2 - $5M per year, with initial revenue anticipated in its fiscal 2025 Q4.
  • Orion will conduct facility lighting audits, install lighting retrofits and provide ongoing maintenance support for the service provider’s customer base.
  • OESX shares were down 2.8% on Monday.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: IPO Roundup: ServiceTitan, Jackson Acquisition II and more
data.description:
As December progresses, the U.S. IPO market remains in its winter slump. However, investors are anticipating the market debut of business management software company ServiceTitan (TTAN) this week.

"The firm’s financials have generated increasing topline revenue, growing gross profit and gross margin, falling but still substantial operating losses and lowered cash used in operations," said Investing Group Leader Donovan Jones in a recent analysis on ServiceTitan.

Upcoming IPOs/SPACs that are expected to be priced this week

  • Fast Track Group (FTRK) is raising $13.5M by offering 3.0M shares at a price range of about $4.00 and $5.00. Description: Event management company.
  • Jackson Acquisition II (JACS.U) is raising $200.0M by offering 20.0M shares at a price of about $10.00. Description: Blank check company.
  • Health in Tech (HIT) is raising $10.4M by offering 2.3M shares at a price range of about $4.00 and $5.00. Description: Insurance technology platform company.
  • Mint Inc (MIMI) is raising $7.9M by offering 1.8M shares at a price range of about $4.00 and $5.00. Description: Interior design services company.
  • NetClass Technology (NTCL) is raising $9.0M by offering 1.8M shares at a price range of about $4.00 and $6.00. Description: SaaS subscription services provider.
  • Park Ha Biological Technology Co. Ltd (PHH) is raising $7.0M by offering 1.2M shares at a price range of about $5.00 and $7.00. Description: Skincare products manufacturer.
  • YSX Tech Co. (YSXT) is raising $6.3M by offering 1.3M shares at a price range of about $4.00 and $6.00. Description: Enterprise business solutions provider.
  • ServiceTitan (TTAN) is raising $479.6M by offering 1.3M shares at a price range of about $4.00 and $6.00. Description: Cloud-based software platform company.
  • Metros Development Co (MTRS) is raising $8.0M by offering 1.3M shares at a price range of about $5.50 and $6.50. Description: Real estate consulting company.
  • New Century Logistics (BVI) is raising $5.6M by offering 1.3M shares at a price range of about $4.00 and $5.00. Description: Air and ocean freight forwarding company.

Filing, terms, and changes

  • Flowco Holdings (FLOC) has filed with the SEC to raise up to $300M in an initial public offering. Description: Energy production equipment maker.
  • Mountain&Sea Health (SYH) has filed with the SEC to raise up to $9M in an initial public offering. Description: Chinese health services company.
  • Stellar V Capital (SVCCU) has filed with the SEC to raise up to $150M in an initial public offering. Description: Blank check company.
  • The Great Restaurant Development Holdings (HPOT) has filed and set terms with the SEC to raise up to $10M in an initial public offering. The company is looking to offer 2.0M shares at a price range of about $4.00 and $6.00. Description: Hotpot restaurant operator.
  • Wellgistics Health (WGRX) has set terms with the SEC to raise up to $4.5M in an initial public offering. The company is looking to offer 0.9M shares at a price range of about $4.50 and $5.50. Description: Pharmaceutical wholesaler.
  • FBS Global (FBGL) has refiled with the SEC to raise up to $11M in an initial public offering. Description: Singapore building services provider.

Withdrawls

  • Luda Technology Group has withdrawn its plans for an initial public offering. Previously, the company had filed with the SEC to raise about $10M by offering 2.5M units at $4.

Lock-up period expirations

  • Perspective Capital Solutions (PCSC) on 12/09/2024. Price movement over the past six months: +0.80%

Since the start of the year, the Renaissance IPO ETF (NYSEARCA:IPO) has risen about 24.5%, slightly underperforming the S&P 500 index, which has gained about 27.7%.

data.title: Mobileye rises after RBC Capital points to company being close to winning a contract
data.description:
Mobileye Global (NASDAQ:MBLY) stock rose about 6% on Monday after RBC Capital said that the company could be close to winning a contract, following the release of a presentation as part of the company's investor event in Munich.

Analyst Tom Narayan said that one of the slides in the presentation could suggest the company is close to a nomination for a Japanese original equipment manufacturer, or OEM. Another slide indicates that the next to nomination stage says "2-3 months." Also included is a European OEM, the company could be close to a nomination for Surround ADAS. A U.S. OEM is noted as within the negotiation and decision-making step. This comes before the nomination step, the analyst added.

Narayan noted that while not an award yet, the stage this Japanese OEM is at, could mean it is close to being won. He added that in their conversations with investors, this is the number one question — if Mobileye will announce an OEM SuperVision, or SV, win.

SuperVision is the company's advanced driver-assist system.

Narayan said they were not expecting a win announcement at the event but the possibility that the company could be close is a positive.

The analyst thinks that the four Japanese OEMs, which could be in the mix, are Honda (HMC), Toyota (TM), Nissan (OTCPK:NSANY) (OTCPK:NSANF) and Mazda (OTCPK:MZDAY) (OTCPK:MZDAF). A Honda or Toyota win could be significant, according to the analyst, due to volumes.

The analyst wonders if the U.S. OEM in the negotiating phase for Surround ADAS could be General Motors (GM) and the European OEM close to nomination for Surround ADAS could be Stellantis (STLA) or Renault (OTCPK:RNSDF).

Mobileye did not respond to a query from Seeking Alpha till the time of publication of this report.

Honda, Toyota, Nissan, General Motors, Stellantis and Renault also did not respond to a query from Seeking Alpha till the time this report was published.

data.title: Eni fuel depot explosion near Florence kills two workers, injuries nine
data.description:
Two people died, nine were injured and three were missing following an explosion and fire Monday at a fuel storage facility owned by Eni (NYSE:E) near Florence, Italy.

The explosion at the Calenzano storage site reportedly occurred in an area were trucks load fuel to be taken to gasoline stations; nearby tanks at the depot were not affected, which the president of the Tuscany region said prevented a much worse tragedy.

Firefighters said the fire was extinguished, but experts from the region's environment protection agency were on site to assess any potential environmental damage.

The prosecutor's office in the nearby town of Prato said it plans to open an investigation to determine criminal responsibility for the incident.

The Calenzano facility is used for gasoline, diesel and jet fuel and is linked by two pipelines to Eni's (E) Livorno oil refinery on the coast.

data.title: Electric vehicle stocks rip big gains after Beijing developments
data.description:
The electric vehicle sector outperformed the broad market in early trading on Monday on a wave of investor confidence tied partially to news from Beijing that China will adopt an "appropriately loose" monetary policy next year and implement fiscal policies aimed at sparking economic growth.

Also in the mix, automakers including Hyundai (OTCPK:HYMTF), VinFast (VFS), and BYD Company (OTCPK:BYDDF) have issued announcements indicating EV plans are still in place, despite concerns over government support.

On Wall Street, a bullish initiation on Rivian Automotive (RIVN) is also easing some concerns that the new administration could be a negative for some EV players.

The EV rally on Monday covered both sides of the Pacific and included some suppliers.

Notable gainers in early trading include Lucid Grouo (LCID) +16.5%, XPeng (XPEV) +16.3%, NIO (NIO) +15.5%, Rivian Automotive (RIVN) +13.1%, Li Auto (LI) +12.3%, Niu Technologies (NIU) +11.8%, Luminar Technologies (LAZR) +11.8%, Mobileye Global (MBLY) +11.5%, ZEEKR Intelligent Technology (ZK) +9.9%, Blink Charging (BLNK) +8.2%, QuantumScape (QS) +8.2%, Polestar Automotive (PSNY) +6.9%, WeRide (WRD) +5.9%, and ChargePoint Holdings (CHPT) +5.4%.

data.title: Weekly economic calendar: Focus on CPI and jobless claims
data.description:
Monday - December 9

10:00 AM Wholesale Inventories (Preliminary)

Wholesale Inventories measure the change in the total value of goods held in inventory by wholesalers. The wholesale inventories are expected to dip 0.1% over the prior month in September.

Tuesday - December 10

6:00 AM NFIB Small Business Optimism

The National Federation of Independent Business Small Business Optimism Index is a composite of ten seasonally adjusted components indicating the health of small businesses in the U.S. The economist expects a reading of 94.5 in November after it rose to 93.7 in October.

8:30 AM Productivity and Cost

Productivity and costs are economic indicators that measure the efficiency of a company's production process and the cost of producing goods and services

1:00 PM 3-Year Note Auction

The auction indicates the percentage yield on three 3-year US Treasury notes. The US Treasury Department will hold an auction for $58 billion of 3-year notes. According to over-the-counter interbank yield quotes for this government bond maturity, the US 3-year note bond yield was 4.08 percent on Monday, December 9.

Wednesday - December 11

7:00 AM MBA Mortgage Applications

The data point measures the change in the number of new applications for mortgages backed by the MBA during the reported week. Mortgage applications rose for the consecutive fourth time to 2.8 for the week ending December 4. The benchmark 30-year fixed mortgage rate dipped 17 bps to 6.69%.

8:30 AM Consumer Price Index

The Consumer Price Index measures the change in the price of goods and services from the perspective of the consumer. It is a key way to measure changes in purchasing trends and inflation. November CPI is expected to edge higher to 2.7% from 2.6% in October. Core CPI, which strips food and energy is expected to be held steady at 3.3%.

10:30 AM EIA Petroleum Inventories

The Energy Information Administration weekly data reports inventory levels of US crude oil, gasoline and distillates stocks. The figure shows how much oil and products are available in storage. U.S. commercial crude oil inventories decreased by 5.07 million barrels in the last week.

1:00 PM 10-Yr Note Auction

The 10-year Treasury yield is the interest rate the U.S. government pays investors who purchase 10-year Treasury notes, also known as T-notes. The treasury will hold an auction of $39 Billion, 10-year note. The yield on the US 10-year Treasury remained below 4.15% on Monday, hovering at seven-week lows as investors awaited crucial inflation data this week, which could influence the Federal Reserve's upcoming interest rate decision.

2:00 PM Treasury Statement

The Monthly Treasury Statement summarizes the financial activities of the federal government and off-budget federal entities and conforms to the Budget of the U.S. Government. The MTS presents a summary of: Receipts and outlays. Surplus or deficit.

Thursday - December 12

8:30 Initial Jobless Claims

Initial Jobless Claims measures the number of individuals who filed for unemployment insurance for the first time during the past week. The jobless claim is expected to dip slightly to 220K after rising to 224K last week.

8:30 AM PPI-Final Demand

The Producer Price Index measures the change in the price of goods sold by manufacturers. It is a leading indicator of consumer price inflation, which accounts for the majority of overall inflation. Core PPI is expected to gain 0.2% month-over-month in November.

10:00 AM Quarterly Service Survey

The Quarterly Services Survey is the only source of service industry indicator performance providing timely estimates of revenue and expenses for selected service industries. The Bureau of Economic Analysis uses QSS data in its estimates of Gross Domestic Product.

10:30 AM EIA Natural Gas Report

The Energy Information Administration Natural Gas Storage report measures the change in the number of cubic feet of natural gas held in underground storage over the prior week. US utilities withdrew 30 billion cubic feet of gas into storage during the week that ended December 5, 2024.

1:00 PM 30-Yr Bond Auction

The auction indicates the percentage yield on 30-year bond auction. The US Treasury Department will hold an auction for $22 billion of 30-year auction. US 30 Year Bond Yield was 4.36 percent on Monday December 9, according to over-the-counter interbank yield quotes for this government bond maturity.

4:30 PM Fed Balance Sheet

The Fed balance sheet is a weekly report listing the assets and liabilities of the Federal Reserve System.

Friday - December 13

8:30 AM Import/Export Prices

The Import/Export Price Indexes contain data on changes in the prices of nonmilitary goods and services traded between the U.S. and the rest of the world. The import and export prices are set to decline by 0.3% and 0.1% in November.

1:00 PM Baker-Hughes Rig Count

The Baker Hughes North American rig count tracks weekly changes in the number of active operating oil & gas rigs. The active rig count acts as a leading indicator of demand for oil products. Crude Oil Rigs in the United States increased to 482 in December 06 from 477 in the previous week.
